behavior_summary: When an actor (player or NPC) enters the script's fast-area/trigger, the handler queries that actor's current map id and sets one of several global "saw*Teleporter" flags to record that the player has 'seen' that teleporter location.

After flagging the appropriate saw* flag(s), the routine iterates a collection of teleporter entries (inferred from a set-info/process-exclude + loop pattern) and spawns TELEPAD instances for each entry, associating each spawn with the entering actor's pid.
Each spawned TELEPAD creation is followed by the engine's suspend/imply control pattern (spawn -> implies/suspend/pop), indicating per-spawn lifecycle or scheduling behavior.
notable_control_flow: Entry is driven by an enter-fast-area event: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch runs when an actor enters the trigger region.

Call to Npc::getMap early in the handler determines the actor's map id; a sequence of comparisons maps concrete ids (0x28, 0x08, 0x32, 0x1F, 0x25, 0x10, 0x1A, 0x0C) to specific saw* global flags which are written.
A loop enumerates one or more teleporter definitions/entries and calls spawn (TELEPAD::0080) with the actor pid and each loop entry pointer to instantiate visible/interactive telepads in the area.
After each spawn there is an implies/suspend/pop pattern (engine scheduling/cleanup) before continuing the loop or finishing.
open_questions: What is the exact source and layout of the collection iterated to spawn TELEPADs (item list, map entity table, or explicit teleporter definition table)? This is inferred but not explicit.
What engine info key does the set info / process exclude sequence set exactly, and how does it affect which entries are enumerated by the loop?
Why do two distinct map ids (0x10 and 0x1A) both map to sawCarthaxLakeTeleporter — is this intentional aliasing of two map variants, or an ambiguous mapping in the original script?

main_function.name: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle an enter-fast-area event: record that the entering actor has 'seen' the local teleporter map by setting a matching saw*Teleporter global, then instantiate TELEPAD objects for teleporter entries present in the trigger area so they become active/visible to the player.
entry_conditions: enterFastArea — invoked when an actor enters the trigger region associated with this script.

function_map.name: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch
function_map.role: enter-fast-area handler that sets saw* flags and spawns TELEPAD instances

function_map.name: Npc::getMap
function_map.role: query the entering actor's current map id (used to decide which saw* flag to set)

function_map.name: spawn (TELEPAD::0080)
function_map.role: create/instantiate a TELEPAD object for a teleporter entry, passing actor pid and teleporter entry pointer

external_calls.purpose: Return the map/region id of the entering actor so the script can set the appropriate saw*Teleporter flag.
Instantiate TELEPAD objects for each teleporter entry found in the trigger area, supplying the player's/process pid and the entry pointer as parameters.

writes.name: sawCentralTenebraeTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x28 (40) to mark this teleporter as seen by the player.

writes.name: sawPlateauTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x08 (8).

writes.name: sawUpperCatacombsTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x32 (50).

writes.name: sawMountainKingTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x1F (31).

writes.name: sawArgentrockIsleTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x25 (37).

writes.name: sawCarthaxLakeTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x10 (16) OR 0x1A (26); appears to alias two map ids to the same flag.

writes.name: sawDaemonsCragTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the entering actor's map id equals 0x0C (12).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When an actor (player or NPC) enters the script's fast-area/trigger, the handler queries that actor's current map id and sets one of several global "saw*Teleporter" flags to record that the player has 'seen' that teleporter location.
